target.name: Form Energy
target.founder: Mateo Jaramillo, Yet-Ming Chiang, Ted Wiley, William Woodford, Marco Ferrara

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $3.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $3.3B - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: TAE Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series G), post-money $6B

data.narrative:
Form Energy, a Series G climate and energy technology company, is evaluated against a universe comprising one public comparable (LNZA) and eight private peers in the same sector, including TAE Technologies, Helion, and Sila Nanotechnologies.

Two triangulation methods are applied. Stage-Progression methodology, anchored to the company's last post-money valuation of $3.5B, applies a typical Series G-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 1.25x, yielding $4.4B. Peer-Median analysis across three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables produces a median valuation of $5.4B, with a range spanning $3.3B (Sila Nanotechnologies, late stage) to $6.0B (TAE Technologies, Series G). The peer-median approach reflects the upper end of recently validated valuations within the climate technology space.

The two-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $3.7B to $6.2B, with a median point of $4.9B. The current $3.5B valuation sits at the lower bound of this range. Key uncertainties include the limited peer sample size (n=3 for median approach), the heterogeneity of stages within the comparables set, and the evolving risk profile typical of late-stage frontier-tech companies. The $4.9B midpoint implies modest expansion from the current post-money valuation, consistent with typical Series G progression dynamics in this sector.
